strengths: 15+ years of continuous full-stack delivery across web, backend, and embedded domains with TypeScript and Node.js present in recent roles.
Hands-on MongoDB and Docker/Kubernetes containerization experience, plus AWS CDK usage at Optilyz, satisfying cloud/infra requirements.
Demonstrated AI-agent and MCP tooling contributions at re:cinq, providing at least surface-level alignment with the AI agent development requirement.
concerns: Nest.js — the primary backend framework for this role — is entirely absent from the resume, skills list, and every employer description; this is the single largest technical gap.

The dominant technology thread across most of the candidate's career is DLang and Ember.js, not the required React/TypeScript/Node stack; React appears only once (Optilyz) and Ember.js is a poor proxy for React proficiency.

Vitest, Playwright, and Kotlin are not listed anywhere in skills or work history; the only notable testing-framework authorship (Trial, Fluent-Asserts) is DLang-specific and yields no transferable signal for the required Jest/Vitest/Playwright ecosystem.
response.justification: Despite a long tenure and genuine breadth, the candidate is a weak fit for this specific role: Nest.js — the core backend framework — is completely unrepresented, the majority of his career was built on DLang and Ember.js rather than the required TypeScript/React/Node stack, and the mandatory testing tools (Vitest, Playwright) alongside the secondary language Kotlin are absent from both his skills and employment history. The recent re:cinq role partially closes the TypeScript and AI-agent gap, but 15 months of partial alignment does not offset a career largely spent on orthogonal technologies, leaving critical primary-stack proficiency undemonstrated.
